text: Pythagorean theorem: for a right triangle with legs a and b and hypotenuse c a^2 + b^2 = c^2
label: math

text: Quadratic formula: the roots of ax^2 + bx + c = 0 are x = (-b ± sqrt(b^2 - 4ac)) / (2a)
label: math

text: Sum of an arithmetic series: S = n(a_1 + a_n) / 2 where n is the number of terms
label: math

text: Sum of a finite geometric series with first term a and common ratio r (r != 1): S = a (1 - r^n) / (1 - r)
label: math

text: Pythagorean trigonometric identity: sin^2(x) + cos^2(x) = 1 for all real x
label: math

text: Euler's identity: e^(i*pi) + 1 = 0 — relates five fundamental constants
label: math

text: Newton's second law: the net force on an object equals its mass times its acceleration F = m a
label: physics

text: Kinetic energy of a moving object: E = (1/2) m v^2
label: physics

text: Period of a simple pendulum (small angle approximation): T = 2 pi sqrt(L/g)
label: physics

text: Hooke's law: an ideal spring exerts a restoring force F = -k x proportional to its displacement from equilibrium
label: physics

text: Ohm's law: the voltage across a resistor equals the product of its current and resistance V = I R
label: physics

text: Coulomb's law: the force between two point charges is F = k q1 q2 / r^2 along the line joining them
label: physics

text: Newton's law of universal gravitation: F = G m1 m2 / r^2 for two point masses
label: physics

text: Kepler's third law: T^2 = (4 pi^2 / (G M)) a^3 — the square of the orbital period scales with the cube of the semi-major axis
label: physics

text: Ideal gas law: P V = n R T relates pressure volume amount and temperature for an ideal gas
label: physics

text: Snell's law: n1 sin(theta1) = n2 sin(theta2) for light crossing a boundary between two media of different refractive index
label: physics

text: Wave relation: the speed of a periodic wave equals frequency times wavelength v = f lambda
label: physics

text: Classical Doppler shift (toward-motion sign convention): f_observed = f (c + v_o) / (c - v_s)
label: physics

text: Einstein's mass-energy equivalence: the rest energy of a body equals its rest mass times the speed of light squared E = m c^2
label: physics

text: Time-independent Schrödinger equation: H psi = E psi — the Hamiltonian acting on a stationary state returns the same state scaled by its energy eigenvalue
label: physics

text: Amylase is an enzyme that catalyzes the hydrolysis of starch into smaller sugars primarily maltose and glucose
label: biology

text: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants algae and some bacteria convert light energy into chemical energy stored in glucose
label: biology

text: DNA is a double-stranded helix of nucleotides whose backbone is alternating sugar and phosphate
label: biology

text: Mitosis is the cell-division process that produces two daughter cells genetically identical to the parent
label: biology

text: Cellular respiration converts glucose and oxygen into carbon dioxide water and ATP
label: biology

text: Insulin is a peptide hormone secreted by beta cells of the pancreatic islets in response to elevated blood glucose
label: biology

text: Mendelian inheritance describes how discrete factors — alleles of genes — pass from parents to offspring
label: biology

text: Evolution by natural selection is the change over generations in the heritable traits of populations
label: biology

text: Antibodies are Y-shaped immunoglobulin proteins produced by B cells that bind specifically to foreign molecules called antigens
label: biology

label: medicine

text: mRNA vaccines deliver a strand of messenger RNA packaged in lipid nanoparticles that instructs the recipient's cells to produce a target antigen — typically a s
label: medicine

label: medicine

text: Hypertension is sustained elevated blood pressure typically defined as systolic pressure of 130 mmHg or higher or diastolic of 80 mmHg or higher
label: medicine

text: Influenza is a respiratory illness caused by influenza A and B viruses whose surface proteins hemagglutinin and neuraminidase mutate rapidly producing seasonal

text: A CT scan reconstructs cross-sectional images of the body from a series of X-ray projections taken at many angles
label: medicine

text: Aspirin (acetylsalicylic acid) inhibits cyclooxygenase enzymes COX-1 and COX-2 reducing prostaglandin synthesis

text: The ABO and Rh systems classify blood by surface antigens on red cells

text: The supply and demand model describes how price and quantity are set in a competitive market
label: economics

text: Inflation is a sustained rise in the general price level usually measured by the change in a price index such as the CPI
label: economics

text: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is the total market value of final goods and services produced within a country in a given period
label: economics

text: A recession is a significant decline in economic activity spread across the economy lasting more than a few months
label: economics

text: Compound interest is interest computed on principal plus accumulated interest so balances grow geometrically
label: economics

text: Opportunity cost is the value of the next-best alternative forgone when a choice is made
label: economics

text: The Federal Reserve is the central bank of the United States
label: economics

text: The stock market is the system of exchanges where shares of public companies are issued and traded
label: economics

text: Antibiotic resistance arises when bacteria carry mutations or acquire genes that let them survive antibiotic exposure
label: medicine

text: Anthropogenic climate change is the long-term shift in global temperature and weather patterns driven mainly by greenhouse gas emissions from burning fossil fue
label: economics

text: Herd immunity occurs when enough of a population is immune to a contagious disease that its transmission slows even among the unvaccinated
label: medicine

text: A market is in equilibrium when the quantity supplied equals the quantity demanded at the prevailing price
label: economics

text: Photosynthesis and cellular respiration are complementary processes that link the biosphere's carbon and oxygen cycles
label: biology

text: The egalitarian view (Rawls) argues that inequalities are justified only if they benefit the worst-off members of society

label: philosophy

text: Substance dualism takes mind and body to be metaphysically distinct kinds
label: philosophy
